frameworks.name: individualized electric-field modeling
frameworks.one_line: MRI-based per-subject E-field magnitude predicts tDCS neurophysiological response

core_question: Do individual electric-field magnitude and anatomical factors explain interindividual variability in tDCS-induced MEP modulation over primary motor cortex?
key_assumptions: MEP amplitude indexes corticospinal excitability changes induced by tDCS
MRI-based finite-element modeling yields a meaningful per-subject estimate of induced E-field
scalp-cortex distance and cortical thickness modulate effective stimulation dose
manipulations: between-subject variation in individual induced electric-field magnitude at M1
key_findings: interindividual variability in MEP modulation after M1 tDCS is substantial
group-level tDCS effect on TMS-MEPs reported previously is revisited at the individual level
individual E-field magnitude and anatomical factors are tested as predictors of MEP change
interpretations: individualized E-field modeling and anatomy may partially account for tDCS response variability
